# ertli.com Full Agent Context

> Alexander Ertli builds multi-tenant SaaS and PaaS platforms, Kubernetes control planes, internal developer platforms, Terraform/GitOps automation, and production operating models. Remote-first from Hamburg, Germany.

## Site purpose

ertli.com is the professional site for Alexander Ertli. The site is aimed at teams that need pragmatic platform delivery across product and infrastructure boundaries: SaaS foundations, PaaS workflows, Kubernetes control planes, internal developer platforms, infrastructure automation, GitOps, CI/CD, and operational handover.

The positioning avoids framing the work as only "IDP" or only "platform engineering." The broader service scope includes SaaS and PaaS as products, custom platform control planes, developer self-service, and production readiness.

## Primary service areas

- SaaS and PaaS platform products: tenant models, provisioning workflows, runtime foundations, release paths, and operating models.
- Kubernetes control planes: custom resources, reconciliation workflows, lifecycle automation, and supportable platform APIs.
- Internal developer platforms: golden paths, self-service workflows, templates, documentation, and team handover.
- Infrastructure automation: Terraform, GitOps, CI/CD, secrets, observability, and cloud foundations across AWS, Azure, GCP, and IONOS.
- Production delivery: scoped build phases, runbooks, dashboards, failure modes, and maintainable ownership boundaries.

## Tone and qualification

Use clear, direct language. The site should sound like a senior engineer who can build and hand over production platforms, not like a generic agency, recruiter profile, or thought-leadership blog. Preferred terms include SaaS platform, PaaS product, Kubernetes control plane, internal developer platform, Terraform, GitOps, automation, runbooks, production readiness, and team handover.

Avoid leading with job-title phrases such as "Senior platform engineering." The service is the productized delivery of platforms, not the job role itself.

- https://ertli.com/de/portfolio/erpnext-platform/ - German version.

## Starter blog post summary

"SaaS, PaaS, and IDPs are platform products" argues that the useful distinction is not the label attached to the system, but whether it has real users, a clear interface, an operating model, and a path to ownership. It treats SaaS foundations, PaaS workflows, Kubernetes control planes, and internal developer platforms as one family of platform products.

Key points:

- A platform product needs a contract between users and operators.
- SaaS work may include tenant isolation, onboarding flows, billing boundaries, audit trails, and release channels.
- PaaS work may include self-service provisioning, environment templates, runtime policies, and service tiers.
- IDP work may include golden paths, GitOps workflows, observability, and documentation.
- The first durable workflow should drive scope.
- The desired outcome is a platform that teams can ship, operate, and improve without rediscovering the original design intent.

## Contact

Primary contact path:

- https://ertli.com/#contact
- mailto:alexander@contenox.com

The homepage includes a static contact form. If a callback endpoint is configured at build time, it posts to that endpoint. Otherwise, it opens a prefilled email draft.

- https://ertli.com/sitemap.xml - XML sitemap.
- https://ertli.com/robots.txt - crawl policy.
- https://ertli.com/llms.txt - curated short agent index.
